1.Name: Riichi
1.Desc: Declare Riichi when your concealed hand is one tile away from winning. Win the hand while in Riichi state.

2.Name: Tanyao (All Simples)
2.Desc: A hand containing no terminals (1s and 9s) or honor tiles.

3.Name: Menzen Tsumo (Concealed Self-Draw)
3.Desc: Win by drawing the winning tile yourself with a fully concealed hand.

4.Name: Yakuhai (Value Tiles): Seat Wind
4.Desc: A hand containing a triplet of the player's seat wind tile.

5.Name: Yakuhai (Value Tiles): Round Wind
5.Desc: A hand containing a triplet of the current round's wind tile.

6.Name: Yakuhai: Dragon Tiles
6.Desc: The hand includes a triplet of any dragon tile (White, Green, or Red).

7.Name: Pinfu (All Sequences, No Points Hand)
7.Desc: A concealed hand with 4 sequences, a non-value pair (not a dragon, seat wind, or round wind), and an open-ended wait for the final tile.

8.Name: Iipeikou (Pure Double Sequence)
8.Desc: The hand contains two identical sequences.

9.Name: Chankan (Robbing a Quad)
9.Desc: Winning on a tile that an opponent uses to add to an existing meld to form a quad (a Thirteen Orphans hand can rob a concealed quad).

10.Name: Rinshan Kaihou (Win from a Dead Wall Draw)
10.Desc: Winning with the replacement tile drawn after making a quad.

11.Name: Haitei Raoyue (Win on the Last Tile Draw)
11.Desc: Winning by drawing the very last tile from the wall.

12.Name: Houtei Raoyui (Win on the Last Discard)
12.Desc: Winning on the very last discarded tile.

13.Name: Ippatsu (One-Shot Win After Riichi)
13.Desc: Winning within one round of turns after declaring Riichi, provided no calls (Pon, Chi, Kan) were made by any player.

14.Name: Dora (Bonus Tile)
14.Desc: The tile following the Dora indicator tile.

15.Name: Aka Dora (Red Dora)
15.Desc: Red 5 of Characters, Red 5 of Dots, and Red 5 of Bamboo.

16.Name: Double Riichi
16.Desc: Declaring Riichi on the first turn of the hand, before any calls (Pon, Chi, Kan) have been made by any player.

17.Name: Sanshoku Doukou (Triple Triplets)
17.Desc: A hand containing a triplet of the same number in all three suits (Characters, Dots, and Bamboo).

18.Name: San Kantsu (Three Quads)
18.Desc: A hand where one player has declared three quads.

19.Name: Toi-Toi (All Triplets)
19.Desc: A hand containing four triplets or quads.

20.Name: San Ankou (Three Concealed Triplets)
20.Desc: A hand containing three concealed triplets.

21.Name: Shou Sangen (Little Three Dragons)
21.Desc: A hand containing triplets of two of the dragon tiles (White, Green, Red) and a pair of the third dragon tile.

22.Name: Honroutou (All Terminals and Honors)
22.Desc: A winning hand composed only of terminals (1s and 9s in Characters, Dots, and Bamboo) and honor tiles.

23.Name: Chiitoitsu (Seven Pairs)
23.Desc: A hand consisting of seven different pairs.

24.Name: Hon Chanta (Mixed Outside Hand)
24.Desc: A hand where every meld (sequence or triplet) and the pair contains at least one terminal (1 or 9) or honor tile.

25.Name: Ittsuu (Pure Straight)
25.Desc: A hand containing sequences of 1-2-3, 4-5-6, and 7-8-9 in the same suit.

26.Name: Sanshoku Doujun (Mixed Triple Sequence)
26.Desc: A hand containing the same sequence in all three suits (Characters, Dots, and Bamboo).

27.Name: Ryanpeikou (Twice Pure Double Sequence)
27.Desc: A hand containing two identical sequences (two Pure Double Sequences).

28.Name: Junchan (Fully Outside Hand)
28.Desc: A hand where every meld (sequence or triplet) and the pair contains at least one terminal tile (1 or 9), with no honor tiles.

29.Name: Honitsu (Half Flush)
29.Desc: A hand composed of tiles from only one suit, including one or more honor tile triplets or a pair.

30.Name: Chinitsu (Full Flush)
30.Desc: A hand composed of tiles from only one suit, with no honor tiles.

31.Name: Nagashi Mangan (Mangan at Draw)
31.Desc: The player's discard pile consists only of terminal and honor tiles at an exhaustive draw, and none of their discards have been called by other players.

32.Name: Tenhou (Blessing of Heaven)
32.Desc: The dealer wins with their initial 14-tile hand.

33.Name: Chiihou (Blessing of Earth)
33.Desc: Win by self-draw on the first turn as a non-dealer, before any calls (Pon, Chi, Kan) are made.

34.Name: Dai Sangen (Big Three Dragons)
34.Desc: A hand containing triplets of all three dragon tiles (White, Green, and Red).

35.Name: Suu Ankou (Four Concealed Triplets)
35.Desc: A hand containing four concealed triplets.

36.Name: Tsuuiisou (All Honors)
36.Desc: A hand composed entirely of honor tiles (winds and dragons).

37.Name: Ryuuiisou (All Green)
37.Desc: A hand composed entirely of green tiles (2, 3, 4, 6, 8 of Bamboo) and the Green Dragon tile (also valid without it).

38.Name: Chinroutou (All Terminals)
38.Desc: A hand containing only terminal tiles (1s and 9s of Characters, Dots, and Bamboo).

39.Name: Kokushi Musou (Thirteen Orphans)
39.Desc: A hand containing one of each terminal and honor tile, plus an additional tile matching any of them.

40.Name: Shousuushii (Little Four Winds)
40.Desc: A hand containing triplets of three wind tiles and a pair of the fourth wind tile.

41.Name: Suukantsu (Four Quads)
41.Desc: A hand won by a single player who has formed four quads.

42.Name: Chuuren Poutou (Nine Gates)
42.Desc: A concealed hand of 1112345678999 in a single suit, plus any other tile from that suit to win.

43.Name: Suu Ankou Tanki (Single-wait Four Concealed Triplets)
43.Desc: A Four Concealed Triplets hand that is completed by forming a pair.

44.Name: Kokushi Musou Juusanmenmachi (Thirteen-sided Wait Thirteen Orphans)
44.Desc: A Kokushi Musou (Thirteen Orphans) hand waiting to win on any of the 13 terminal or honor tiles.

45.Name: Junsei Chuuren Poutou (True Nine Gates)
45.Desc: A Nine Gates hand waiting to win on any of the 9 possible tiles.

46.Name: Dai Suushi (Big Four Winds)
46.Desc: A hand containing triplets of all four wind tiles.

47.Name: Suufon Renda (Abortive Draw on Four Winds)
47.Desc: An abortive draw when all four players discard the same wind tile on their first turn, with no calls made.

48.Name: Suukaikan (Abortive Draw on Four Quads)
48.Desc: An abortive draw when a total of four quads are declared by two or more players.

49.Name: Kyuushu Kyuuhai (Abortive Draw on Nine Different Terminals/Honors)
49.Desc: A player can declare an abortive draw if their starting hand contains nine or more different terminal or honor tiles, provided no calls have been made before their first turn.

50.Name: Suucha Riichi (Abortive Draw on Four Riichis)
50.Desc: An abortive draw when all four players have successfully declared Riichi.
